mnemonics.title: Mnemonic 1
mnemonics.content: Markov Moves: Memoryless Means My Past Doesn't Predict My Present.

mnemonics.title: Mnemonic 2
mnemonics.content: Joint Journeys: JPDs Justify Probability Dependencies.

mnemonics.title: Key Point 1
mnemonics.content: A stochastic process is a collection of random variables indexed by time, representing the evolution of a random system.

mnemonics.title: Key Point 2
mnemonics.content: The Markov Property states that the future state depends only on the present state, not on the full history (the process is 'memoryless').

mnemonics.title: Key Point 3
mnemonics.content: The Joint Probability Distribution gives the probability that multiple random variables take on specific values simultaneously, defining their relationship.
